articles.title: Tesla Is Quietly Becoming a Global Utility and Wall Street Barely Notices
articles.summary: Tesla's energy business, which generated nearly $12.8 billion in revenue in 2025, is rapidly growing and transforming the company into a global utility, yet Wall Street continues to primarily value it as a car manufacturer. With significant advancements in Megapack deployments, LFP battery production, and software-driven solutions like Autobidder and Virtual Power Plant, Tesla is building a comprehensive energy ecosystem. This shift is largely unrecognized by the market, which remains focused on automotive sales and delivery numbers.
articles.text_to_analyze: Tesla Is Quietly Becoming a Global Utility and Wall Street Barely Notices. This shift is largely unrecognized by the market, which remains focused on automotive sales and delivery numbers.

articles.title: Tesla's South Korean sales up more than 300% to 11,134 vehicles in March
articles.summary: Tesla's car registrations in South Korea surged by 330% to 11,134 vehicles in March compared to the previous year, according to market researcher Carisyou. This significant increase follows Tesla's recent price cuts for its Model Y and Model 3 electric vehicles manufactured in China, intensifying price competition among EV makers in Korea.

articles.title: Rocket Lab, Beyond Meat, Micron Technology And More: 5 Stocks Investors Couldn't Stop Buzzing About This Week
articles.summary: This article highlights five stocks—Tesla, Micron Technology, Rocket Lab, Nike, and Beyond Meat—that were trending among retail investors from March 30 to April 2 due to various factors like earnings reports, AI developments, and corporate news. Tesla missed delivery expectations, Micron faced volatility over an AI algorithm, Rocket Lab received regulatory approval for an acquisition, Nike plunged despite beating estimates, and Beyond Meat saw revenue decline amid weak demand.

articles.title: SMOG ETF Volume Surges as Tesla, Rivian Diverge
articles.summary: The VanEck Low Carbon Energy ETF (SMOG) has experienced significantly higher trading volume than its daily average. This surge comes as two of its largest holdings, Tesla (TSLA) and Rivian (RIVN), show divergent performances and news. Tesla's stock is pressured after missing Q1 2026 delivery forecasts, while Rivian saw a 20% increase in deliveries, beat estimates, and secured a major investment and partnership with Uber.

articles.title: Tesla Stock Keeps Falling - But 10% Lower TSLA Puts Yield 2.0% for One Month Out
articles.summary: Tesla Inc (TSLA) stock has been declining, driving up put option premiums. This presents an attractive opportunity for short-sellers and value investors. Using short out-of-the-money (OTM) puts allows investors to profit from the elevated premiums and potentially buy TSLA shares at a lower price point, with a 10% lower strike price put yielding 2.0% for one month out.

articles.title: Weekend Round-Up: Tesla's Q1 Miss, Uber's Robotaxi Ambitions, Baidu's AV Safety Concerns, Ford's Bluecrui
articles.summary: This article summarizes a dynamic week in the tech and auto sectors, highlighting Tesla's missed Q1 delivery target and Uber's potential integration of Tesla Robotaxis. It also covers safety concerns regarding Baidu's Apollo Go Robotaxis, Ford's BlueCruise system being implicated in fatal collisions, and American Airlines' discussions with SpaceX for in-flight Wi-Fi.

articles.title: Tesla Stock Keeps Falling - But 10% Lower TSLA Puts Yield 2.0% for One Month Out
articles.summary: Tesla (TSLA) stock has seen a recent decline, pushing up put option premiums. This situation allows investors to sell out-of-the-money (OTM) puts, achieving attractive yields while setting a lower potential buy-in point for the stock. Despite the recent dip, analysts still maintain significantly higher price targets for TSLA, suggesting potential upside for long-term investors.

articles.title: Dad, Son Burned to Death in Tesla After Battery System Fire, Lawsuit Claims
articles.summary: A mother has filed a wrongful death lawsuit against Tesla after her teenage son and his father were burned to death in a fiery Tesla Model 3 crash in Georgia in December 2024. The lawsuit alleges that the car's doors malfunctioned, trapping the occupants, and claims design flaws in Tesla's Autopilot system and battery pack contributed to the incident. The complaint seeks damages for the son's suffering and death, along with legal fees and funeral expenses.
articles.text_to_analyze: Dad, Son Burned to Death in Tesla After Battery System Fire, Lawsuit Claims. The lawsuit alleges that the car's doors malfunctioned, trapping the occupants, and claims design flaws in Tesla's Autopilot system and battery pack contributed to the incident. The complaint seeks damages for the son's suffering and death, along with legal fees and funeral expenses.

articles.title: Eyes on AI! Wedbush Keeps $600 Tesla Stock Price Target despite Q1 Miss
articles.summary: Despite Tesla missing Wall Street's Q1 delivery estimates for the second consecutive quarter and falling short on energy storage deployment, Wedbush analyst Dan Ives maintained his Buy rating and $600 price target for TSLA stock. Ives attributes his bullish outlook to Tesla's strong AI push and robotaxi plans, projecting significant future revenue from these areas. While acknowledging challenges in Europe, Ives noted strong growth in China and emphasized the company's long-term strategy centered on AI and robotics investments.

articles.title: Sky Quarry, Sidus Space, Transocean, United Airlines And Tesla: Why These 5 Stocks Are On Investors' Radars Today
articles.summary: This article highlights five stocks—Sky Quarry, Sidus Space, Transocean, United Airlines, and Tesla—that captured investor attention. Sky Quarry and Sidus Space saw significant gains due to company-specific news regarding crude production and NASA ties, respectively. Transocean also rose on new drilling contracts and debt reduction, while United Airlines and Tesla experienced declines due to concerns over rising fuel costs and lower-than-expected delivery figures, respectively.

articles.title: SMOG ETF Volume Surges 17x as Tesla Slumps
articles.summary: The VanEck Low Carbon Energy ETF (SMOG) experienced an unusual trading volume, surging 17 times its daily average. This surge coincides with Tesla's weaker-than-expected Q1 2026 deliveries, which pressured the stock and led analysts to cut price targets despite long-term AI and robotaxi ambitions. In contrast, Rivian, a smaller holding, reported strong delivery growth and received more optimistic analyst commentary due to its R2 platform and Uber partnership.

articles.title: Tesla Rival Rivian's US Sales Decline For Fourth Consecutive Month Ahead Of R2 Launch
articles.summary: Rivian Automotive Inc.'s US sales have declined for the fourth consecutive month, with a significant year-over-year drop in the first quarter of 2026. This comes as the company prepares to launch its new R2 Crossover SUV, which is expected to boost sales, and as it signed a significant deal with Uber for R2 Robotaxis. Rivian also unveiled its Universal Hands Free assisted driving system, designed to compete with Tesla's FSD.

Rivian also unveiled its Universal Hands Free assisted driving system, designed to compete with Tesla's FSD.

articles.title: Tesla Rival Rivian's US Sales Decline For Fourth Consecutive Month Ahead Of R2 Launch
articles.summary: Rivian Automotive Inc. has experienced a consecutive four-month decline in U.S. sales, with Q1 2026 sales falling by over 26.5% year-over-year. This comes as the company prepares to launch its R2 Crossover SUV, which is expected to boost sales, with 20,000 to 25,000 units anticipated in 2026. Rivian also recently signed a $1.25 billion deal with Uber for R2 Robotaxis and unveiled its Universal Hands Free assisted driving system.

articles.title: YieldMax Short TSLA Option Income Strategy ETF announces weekly distribution of $0.3311
articles.summary: The YieldMax Short TSLA Option Income Strategy ETF (TSLY) has announced a weekly distribution of $0.3311 per share. This distribution is payable on April 12, 2024, to shareholders of record as of April 10, 2024. The ex-dividend date for this distribution is April 10, 2024.

articles.title: First Solar’s New U.S. Facility And Tesla Shift Utility Scale Solar Economics
articles.summary: First Solar (FSLR) plans a new 3.7GW U.S. finishing facility to reduce tariff exposure and align with domestic incentives, shifting production from Southeast Asia. Concurrently, Tesla is entering U.S. solar manufacturing equipment, potentially altering market dynamics. These developments could impact First Solar's cost structure, competitive position, and the broader utility-scale solar industry.

articles.title: Bloomberg: Tesla’s AI dreams don’t change reality, cars still pay the bills
articles.summary: Bloomberg's Craig observed that Tesla's future ambitions, such as autonomous vehicles and humanoid robots, are still theoretical, while the company's core automotive business recently missed delivery expectations for Q1 2026. Tesla delivered 358,000 units against a projected 372,000, signaling a dependency on car sales despite significant investments in AI and R&D. The company's high valuation presumes success in these future ventures, but current market predictions show low confidence in their near-term realization, making Tesla vulnerable if automotive sales decline further.

articles.title: 9 Consumer Discretionary Stocks Whale Activity In Today's Session
articles.summary: This article highlights nine consumer discretionary stocks that experienced significant "whale" option trading activity during today's session. It details specific call option trades for companies like Tesla, Norwegian Cruise Line, Carvana, Home Depot, DoorDash, Restaurant Brands International, Wingstop, Ollie's Bargain Outlet, and Booking Holdings, including strike prices, expiration dates, and sentiment. The article serves as a guide for traders looking to identify potential opportunities based on large-scale options movements.

articles.title: Tesla Rival Rivian's US Sales Decline For Fourth Consecutive Month Ahead Of R2 Launch
articles.summary: Rivian Automotive Inc. experienced its fourth consecutive month of declining U.S. sales, with a 26.5% year-over-year drop in Q1 2026 sales, totaling 8,141 vehicles. This decline precedes the highly anticipated launch of its R2 Crossover SUV, which is expected to boost sales and is priced significantly lower than its current premium models. Rivian also has a $1.25 billion deal with Uber for R2 Robotaxis and is developing its Universal Hands Free assisted driving system to compete with Tesla's FSD.
